name: Unofficial Well-Architected MAS MindForge Lens
description: A custom AWS Well-Architected Lens that maps the MAS Project MindForge AI Risk Management and Governance Framework (January 2026) into the AWS Well-Architected Tool. This lens helps Financial Services Institutions assess their AI governance posture across four domains: AI Oversight, AI Risk Management, AI Lifecycle Management, and Enablers. It is unofficial and community-maintained; always refer to the official MAS handbook for authoritative guidance.

questions.title: How do you define the scope and application of your AI governance framework?
questions.description: Establish clear boundaries for which AI systems, use cases, and business units fall under governance. Define materiality thresholds and classification criteria based on the MindForge AI Risk Taxonomy.

choices.title: Comprehensive scope defined with materiality thresholds and documented exclusions

improvementPlan.displayText: Maintain and annually review scope documentation. Ensure new AI use cases are automatically assessed against materiality criteria.

choices.title: Partial scope defined for some AI systems or business units only

improvementPlan.displayText: Expand scope definition to cover all AI systems, including third-party and vendor solutions. Define materiality thresholds for all use cases.

choices.title: None of these

improvementPlan.displayText: Define governance scope immediately. Start with a risk-based inventory of all AI systems and classify by materiality.

questions.title: How do you define and assign responsibilities for AI oversight?
questions.description: Establish the three lines of defence model for AI governance. Define roles, accountability, and escalation paths for AI risk decisions.

choices.title: Three lines of defence model implemented with documented roles and accountability

improvementPlan.displayText: Conduct annual training on the three lines of defence. Review and update role definitions as AI use cases evolve.

choices.title: Some oversight roles defined but gaps remain in accountability or escalation

improvementPlan.displayText: Formalise the three lines of defence model. Assign explicit accountability for AI risk decisions to named individuals.

choices.title: None of these

improvementPlan.displayText: Define AI oversight responsibilities using the three lines of defence model. Start with a RACI matrix for AI governance.

questions.title: How do you ensure effective AI-related policies, procedures, and standards?
questions.description: Develop and maintain documented policies covering AI ethics, risk appetite, model governance, data usage, and regulatory compliance.

choices.title: Comprehensive AI policy framework approved by board and regularly reviewed

improvementPlan.displayText: Schedule annual policy reviews. Track regulatory changes and update policies proactively.

choices.title: Some AI policies exist but are incomplete or not board-approved

improvementPlan.displayText: Develop a complete AI policy framework covering ethics, risk appetite, model governance, and data usage. Obtain board approval.

choices.title: None of these

improvementPlan.displayText: Draft an AI policy framework starting with high-risk use cases. Use the MAS MindForge handbook as a reference.

questions.title: How do you enhance organisation-level AI risk management?
questions.description: Integrate AI risk into the enterprise risk management framework. Establish AI-specific risk appetite, tolerances, and reporting mechanisms.

choices.title: AI risk fully integrated into ERM with defined appetite and regular reporting

improvementPlan.displayText: Enhance AI risk reporting with predictive indicators. Stress-test AI risk scenarios annually.

choices.title: AI risk partially integrated or siloed from broader enterprise risk

improvementPlan.displayText: Formalise AI risk integration into the ERM framework. Define AI-specific risk appetite and reporting cadence.

choices.title: None of these

improvementPlan.displayText: Map AI risks to the enterprise risk taxonomy. Establish an AI risk register and define appetite thresholds.

questions.title: How do you manage third-party AI risks?
questions.description: Assess and monitor AI risks from vendors, cloud providers, and outsourced AI services. Ensure contractual safeguards and due diligence.

choices.title: Third-party AI risk framework with due diligence, contracts, and ongoing monitoring

improvementPlan.displayText: Conduct periodic third-party AI risk audits. Update contractual safeguards as vendor capabilities evolve.

choices.title: Some third-party AI risks assessed but no systematic framework

improvementPlan.displayText: Build a systematic third-party AI risk framework. Include AI-specific clauses in all vendor contracts.

choices.title: None of these

improvementPlan.displayText: Inventory all third-party AI services. Conduct risk assessments and establish minimum contractual safeguards.

questions.title: How do you enhance use case-level AI risk management?
questions.description: Conduct structured risk assessments for each AI use case. Document inherent risk, residual risk, and control effectiveness.

choices.title: All AI use cases have documented risk assessments with inherent and residual risk scores

improvementPlan.displayText: Enhance use case assessments with scenario analysis and stress testing. Link to enterprise risk reporting.

choices.title: Some use cases assessed but not all, or assessments lack residual risk analysis

improvementPlan.displayText: Extend risk assessments to all AI use cases. Ensure each assessment covers inherent risk, controls, and residual risk.

choices.title: None of these

improvementPlan.displayText: Start with the highest-materiality use cases. Use the MindForge AI Card template to structure assessments.

questions.title: How do you ensure AI inventory capabilities?
questions.description: Maintain an accurate, up-to-date inventory of all AI systems, models, data sources, and dependencies across the organisation.

choices.title: Centralised AI inventory with automated discovery and regular reconciliation

improvementPlan.displayText: Enhance inventory with lineage tracking and automated change detection. Integrate with model registries.

choices.title: Partial inventory exists but is manual, incomplete, or not regularly updated

improvementPlan.displayText: Implement a centralised AI inventory system. Automate discovery where possible and establish quarterly reconciliation.

choices.title: None of these

improvementPlan.displayText: Create a baseline AI inventory starting with known high-risk systems. Establish ownership and update cadence.

questions.title: How do you manage use case context and design?
questions.description: Document business objectives, success criteria, stakeholder requirements, and ethical considerations before AI system design.

choices.title: All AI use cases have documented context, objectives, success criteria, and ethical review

improvementPlan.displayText: Enhance design documentation with bias analysis and fairness metrics. Integrate ethical review into design gates.

choices.title: Some use cases have design documentation but ethical review is inconsistent

improvementPlan.displayText: Standardise use case context documentation for all AI systems. Mandate ethical review before design approval.

choices.title: None of these

improvementPlan.displayText: Create a use case context template covering objectives, stakeholders, success criteria, and ethical considerations.

questions.title: How do you manage data acquisition and processing for AI?
questions.description: Ensure data quality, provenance, privacy compliance, and bias mitigation across the AI data pipeline.

choices.title: Data governance framework with quality metrics, provenance tracking, and bias monitoring

improvementPlan.displayText: Enhance data governance with automated quality checks and real-time bias detection. Expand provenance to third-party data.

choices.title: Some data controls exist but gaps in quality, provenance, or bias management

improvementPlan.displayText: Build a comprehensive data governance framework for AI. Implement data quality metrics and bias monitoring.

choices.title: None of these

improvementPlan.displayText: Start with data inventory and classification. Implement basic quality checks and establish data ownership.

questions.title: How do you manage onboarding, build, and review of AI systems?
questions.description: Establish development standards, model validation, testing protocols, and review gates for AI system build phases.

choices.title: Structured build process with validation, testing, peer review, and model cards

improvementPlan.displayText: Enhance build process with automated testing and continuous validation. Integrate model cards into the release process.

choices.title: Some build controls exist but validation or review is inconsistent

improvementPlan.displayText: Formalise the AI build process with mandatory validation, testing, and peer review gates. Introduce model cards.

choices.title: None of these

improvementPlan.displayText: Define minimum build standards for AI. Implement model validation and basic testing before deployment.

questions.title: How do you manage AI system deployment?
questions.description: Implement deployment controls including staging environments, rollback capabilities, A/B testing, and production readiness checks.

choices.title: Deployment pipeline with staging, rollback, readiness checks, and automated monitoring

improvementPlan.displayText: Enhance deployment with canary releases and automated rollback triggers. Integrate deployment checks into CI/CD.

choices.title: Some deployment controls exist but pipeline is manual or lacks rollback

improvementPlan.displayText: Build a structured deployment pipeline for AI. Implement staging, readiness checks, and rollback capabilities.

choices.title: None of these

improvementPlan.displayText: Define minimum deployment controls for AI. Start with staging environment and basic readiness checklist.

questions.title: How do you manage AI system usage, monitoring, and change?
questions.description: Continuously monitor AI system performance, drift, fairness, and security. Establish change management for model updates.

choices.title: Continuous monitoring with drift detection, fairness metrics, and formal change management

improvementPlan.displayText: Enhance monitoring with automated alerting and model retraining triggers. Integrate change management with model registry.

choices.title: Some monitoring exists but lacks drift detection or formal change management

improvementPlan.displayText: Implement continuous monitoring for all production AI systems. Add drift detection and formalise change management.

choices.title: None of these

improvementPlan.displayText: Start with basic performance monitoring for production AI. Define change management process for model updates.

questions.title: How do you enable AI governance with skills, knowledge, and culture?
questions.description: Build organisational capability through training, awareness programs, and a culture of responsible AI use.

choices.title: Comprehensive AI training program with role-based curricula and culture metrics

improvementPlan.displayText: Expand training to emerging AI topics. Measure and report on culture and awareness metrics.

choices.title: Some training exists but is not role-based or lacks coverage of AI risk

improvementPlan.displayText: Develop role-based AI training curricula. Add AI risk and ethics modules for all relevant roles.

choices.title: None of these

improvementPlan.displayText: Start with awareness training for leadership and AI practitioners. Build towards role-based curricula.

questions.title: How do you manage AI infrastructure?
questions.description: Ensure compute, storage, and network infrastructure supports AI workloads securely, scalably, and cost-effectively.

choices.title: AI infrastructure managed with security controls, scalability, and cost governance

improvementPlan.displayText: Optimise infrastructure costs with auto-scaling and spot instances. Enhance security with zero-trust networking.

choices.title: Some infrastructure controls exist but gaps in security, scalability, or cost management

improvementPlan.displayText: Formalise AI infrastructure governance. Implement security controls, auto-scaling, and cost monitoring.

choices.title: None of these

improvementPlan.displayText: Assess current AI infrastructure against security and scalability requirements. Establish baseline controls.
